title: init(client:bucketName:keyPrefix:)
rawIndexableTextContent: Creates an S3 cloud file system.  A configured S3Client with valid credentials. The S3 bucket to use for storage. Optional prefix for all keys. Defaults to empty string. A trailing “/” is added automatically if not present.
summary: Creates an S3 cloud file system.

title: connect()
rawIndexableTextContent: Attempts to connect to the cloud backend.
summary: Attempts to connect to the cloud backend.

title: S3CloudFileSystem
rawIndexableTextContent: A cloud file system backed by Amazon S3, using the AWS SDK for Swift.  Overview This backend requires the AWSS3 product from aws-sdk-swift. With the source distribution, enable the S3 package trait and SPM resolves it automatically. With the binary distribution (XCFrameworks), add aws-sdk-swift as a separate package dependency in your project. S3 is a flat key-value store, not a hierarchical file system. This backend simulates directory semantics using key prefixes and / delimiters — the standard S3 convention. For example, the Ensembles path ensembleId/events/data.json maps to the S3 key <keyPrefix>ensembleId/events/data.json. Directory creation writes a zero-byte marker object with a trailing / key. Directory listing uses ListObjectsV2 with a delimiter to discover “subdirectories” via common prefixes. This approach is compatible with S3 console browsing and third-party tools. The app configures an S3Client with its own credentials and injects it at init time:  Authentication The AWS SDK for Swift resolves credentials automatically from the standard credential provider chain. Configure credentials using any of: Environment variables: AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, and optionally AWS_SESSION_TOKEN Shared credentials file: ~/.aws/credentials (set via aws configure) IAM role: Automatic on EC2, ECS, and Lambda SSO: Run aws sso login, then configure ~/.aws/config  Compatible Services This backend works with any S3-compatible service by configuring a custom endpoint on the S3Client: MinIO — self-hosted object storage Cloudflare R2 — zero egress fees Backblaze B2 — low-cost cloud storage DigitalOcean Spaces — simple object storage  Threading This class is @unchecked Sendable. The S3Client handles its own thread safety internally. Mutable state (loggedIn) is only written during connect() and error handling.
summary: A cloud file system backed by Amazon S3, using the AWS SDK for Swift.

title: EnsemblesS3
rawIndexableTextContent: An Amazon S3-based cloud backend for Ensembles. Overview EnsemblesS3 provides S3CloudFileSystem, an implementation of the Ensembles CloudFileSystem protocol that stores sync data in an Amazon S3 bucket. It uses the official AWS SDK for Swift for all API operations. S3 is a natural fit for Ensembles — it’s highly durable, globally available, and cost-effective for the small files that Ensembles exchanges. S3-compatible services (MinIO, DigitalOcean Spaces, Backblaze B2, Cloudflare R2) can also be used by configuring the S3Client with a custom endpoint. S3 as a File System S3 is a flat key-value store, not a hierarchical file system. S3CloudFileSystem simulates directory semantics using key prefixes and / delimiters — the standard S3 convention. For example, the Ensembles path ensembleId/events/data.json maps to the S3 key prefix/ensembleId/events/data.json. Directory creation writes a zero-byte marker object with a trailing / in the key. Directory listing uses ListObjectsV2 with a delimiter to discover “subdirectories” via common prefixes. Authentication The AWS SDK for Swift supports multiple credential sources (environment variables, IAM roles, SSO, credential files). Your app creates and configures an S3Client, then injects it:  Setup  Key Prefix The keyPrefix parameter isolates Ensembles data within a shared bucket. For example, with keyPrefix: "ensembles/", the path /MyStore/events/abc.json maps to the S3 key ensembles/MyStore/events/abc.json. A trailing / is added automatically if omitted. If keyPrefix is empty (the default), Ensembles data is stored at the bucket root. S3-Compatible Services To use an S3-compatible service, configure the S3Client with a custom endpoint:  Error Handling AWS SDK errors are mapped to EnsembleError cases: AccessDenied, InvalidAccessKeyId, and SignatureDoesNotMatch map to EnsembleError/authenticationFailure. NoSuchKey, NoSuchBucket, and NotFound map to EnsembleError/fileAccessFailed. All other errors map to EnsembleError/serverError. Dependencies This module depends on the AWS SDK for Swift (AWSS3 module).
summary: An Amazon S3-based cloud backend for Ensembles.

title: downloadFile(atPath:toLocalFile:)
rawIndexableTextContent: Downloads a file from the cloud file system to a local path.  The path of the file in the cloud file system. The destination path on the local device.
summary: Downloads a file from the cloud file system to a local path.

title: fileExists(atPath:)
rawIndexableTextContent: Determines whether a file exists at the given path, and whether it is a directory.
summary: Determines whether a file exists at the given path, and whether it is a directory.

title: contentsOfDirectory(atPath:)
rawIndexableTextContent: Returns the contents of a directory at the given path as an array of CloudItem values.
summary: Returns the contents of a directory at the given path as an array of CloudItem values.

title: removeItem(atPath:)
rawIndexableTextContent: Removes the item (file or directory) at the given path.
summary: Removes the item (file or directory) at the given path.

title: fetchUserIdentity()
rawIndexableTextContent: Fetches a token representing the current user of the cloud file system. Often this will be the user or login name.
summary: Fetches a token representing the current user of the cloud file system. Often this will be the user or login name.

title: isConnected
rawIndexableTextContent: Whether the file system is connected and ready for file transactions.
summary: Whether the file system is connected and ready for file transactions.

title: keyPrefix
rawIndexableTextContent: Optional key prefix within the bucket (e.g. “ensembles/”). If set, all keys are prefixed with this value.
summary: Optional key prefix within the bucket (e.g. “ensembles/”). If set, all keys are prefixed with this value.
